title: Context Match Request
description: Sent by publisher to router or provider to evaluate packages against contextual signals. The provider uses its synced package set for the placement. MUST NOT contain user identity. The request_id MUST NOT correlate with any identity match request_id.

protocol_version.description: TMP protocol version. Allows receivers to handle semantic differences across versions.

request_id.description: Unique request identifier. MUST NOT correlate with any identity match request_id.

property_rid.description: Property catalog UUID (UUID v7). Globally unique, stable identifier assigned by the property catalog. The primary key for TMP matching and property list targeting.

property_id.description: Publisher's human-readable property slug (e.g., 'cnn_homepage'). Optional when property_rid is present. Useful for logging and debugging.

placement_id.description: Placement identifier from the property's registered placements. Identifies where on the property this ad opportunity exists. One placement per request.

artifacts.description: Public content references adjacent to this ad opportunity. Each artifact identifies content via a public identifier the buyer can resolve independently — no private registry sync required.

type.description: Identifier type. 'url' for web pages, 'url_hash' for URL-addressable content the publisher prefers not to share directly (buyer matches against pre-crawled index), 'eidr' for film/TV (EIDR DOI), 'gracenote' for music/TV (Gracenote TMS ID), 'rss_guid' for podcast episodes (RSS GUID), 'isbn' for books, 'custom' for publisher-defined identifiers.

value.description: The identifier value. For 'url': the canonical content URL. For 'url_hash': Blake3 hash of the canonicalized URL, base64-encoded. For 'eidr': the EIDR DOI (e.g., '10.5240/xxxx'). For 'rss_guid': the episode GUID from the RSS feed. For 'custom': a publisher-defined identifier.

geo.description: Geographic context for this impression. Publisher controls granularity — country is sufficient for volume filtering and regulatory compliance, finer granularity helps with valuation. This describes where the ad is serving, not where the user is (user location is identity data). All fields optional.

metro.description: Metro area, using the same classification systems as AdCP targeting.

value.description: Metro code within the system (e.g., '501' for New York DMA).

context_signals.description: Pre-computed classifier outputs for the content environment. Use when artifact pre-registration is not feasible (e.g., real-time conversation turns, search queries) or to supplement artifact-based matching. Raw content (conversation text, article body, URLs) MUST NOT be included — only classified outputs. The publisher is the classifier boundary.

topics.description: Content topic identifiers. Use IAB Content Taxonomy 3.0 IDs (e.g., '632' for Food & Drink) when taxonomy_id is 7, or human-readable strings (e.g., 'cooking.pasta') for custom taxonomies.

taxonomy_source.description: Organization that defines the topic taxonomy. Default: 'iab'. Other sources can be added as the ecosystem evolves.

taxonomy_id.description: Taxonomy version within the source. For IAB, follows the AdCOM cattax enum: 7 = Content Taxonomy 3.0. Default: 7.

keywords.description: Content keywords extracted by the publisher's classifier.

content_policies.description: Policy IDs from the AdCP policy registry that this content satisfies (e.g., 'common_sense_brand_safety' for Common Sense Brand Standards baseline). Buyers filter on policies they require. An empty array means no policies have been evaluated.

summary.description: Publisher-generated natural language summary of the content for relevance judgment (e.g., 'User exploring Italian cookware options for home pasta making'). Useful for LLM-native buyers that evaluate relevance semantically.

embedding.description: Content embedding as base64-encoded int8 vector. Captures semantic content beyond what topics and keywords express. Publishers declare the model used. For standardized matching, use the protocol-recommended model (nomic-embed-text-v1.5, 256 dims, int8 quantized = 256 bytes).

embedding_model.description: Embedding model identifier (e.g., 'nomic-embed-text-v1.5'). Required when embedding is present.

embedding_dims.description: Number of dimensions in the embedding vector. Required when embedding is present.

package_ids.description: Restrict evaluation to specific packages. When omitted, the provider evaluates all eligible packages for this placement (the common case). MUST NOT vary by user — the same package_ids must be sent for every user on a given placement. User-dependent filtering leaks identity into the context path.
